front: Play: Strepsiades enrolls son Pheidippides at Socrates's school, the Thinkery, to learn how to avoid paying debts from buying horses
Play: ends with Strepsiades and slave Xanthias burning down the Thinkery after Pheidippides beats his father
Play: Right Logic (Superior Argument) debates Wrong Logic (Inferior Argument); the worse argument wins
Play: Socrates sits in a basket floating in the air, says he doesn't want his mind to dry out like lettuce
Play: a lizard defecates on him while he contemplates the moon
Play: women swear an oath on a skin of Thasian wine, vowing not to 'crouch like a lioness on all fours,' to end the Peloponnesian War
Play: Dionysus travels to the underworld to bring back Euripides but ends up choosing Aeschylus
Play: trades clothes with slave Xanthias to avoid being mistaken for Heracles
Play: chorus chants 'brekekek koax koax'
Play: 1974 Yale musical used the varsity swim team as the chorus
Play: Pisthetaerus creates the city of Cloudcuckooland to blockade the gods from receiving human offerings
Play: Prometheus sneaks in under a parasol
Play: Philocleon is addicted to jury service
Play: son Anticleon tries to cure him
Play: mock trial of a dog for stealing Sicilian cheese
Play: set at the Thesmophoria festival
Play: women discuss Euripides's portrayal of women
Play: contains the first documented use of the word 'mimesis'
Play: Praxagora leads women to disguise as men, vote themselves into power, and impose communal property
Play: Trygaeus rides a giant dung beetle to an audience with the gods to free the goddess Peace; marries Harvest
